title: REAPERS — ROGUE OPERATIVE GROUP
body:
*ORIGINATING DIVISION:* COUNTER-OPERATIONS, THREAT ASSESSMENT BRANCH.
*REVIEW STATUS:* <green>ACTIVE<green>

^SUBJECT: COLLECTIVE THREAT ASSESSMENT — ROGUE OPERATIVE GROUP^
*DESIGNATION: REAPERS*
*GROUP STATUS:* ACTIVE — UNCONTAINED

^OVERVIEW^
Intelligence has confirmed the existence of a structured rogue operative cell operating under the informal designation "Reapers." The group is assessed as responsible for a series of coordinated attacks against ECAF infrastructure initiated no earlier than late 2047. All confirmed and probable members share prior affiliation with the UE-JTF, specifically personnel connected to Team 11 operations and the Rozhia incident of 13 November 2031.
The group does not operate under any identified national, political, or ideological mandate. No communication of demands or grievances has been recorded. Operational targeting is assessed as deliberate and personal in nature, directed specifically at ECAF institutional assets and personnel connected to Founder Rodriguez.
Individual dossiers for confirmed members are filed separately under their respective HERMES reference numbers. This document consolidates group-level threat assessment only.

ORIGIN — THE KAZAKHSTAN INCIDENT, 2031
The Reapers did not form as a deliberate organizational act. Current assessment indicates the group's existence is a direct downstream consequence of the unauthorized operation led by then-Master Sergeant Samuel Collins in November 2031.
Collins, operating without command authorization, mobilized a splinter element of UEJTF Team 11 operators he assessed as personally reliable — among them LIBRA, Gavalas, and Seale — to conduct an unsanctioned assault on a suspected development facility in Kazakhstan. The operation resulted in the facility's destruction and the confirmed or presumed deaths of all participants. Collins survived, was extracted, court-martialed, and subsequently imprisoned.
The three operators listed as KIA in the aftermath — LIBRA, Gavalas, and Seale — did not die. Their survival was not reported. Each disappeared independently from NATO tracking infrastructure following the incident.
ECAF's current assessment is that the Kazakhstan operation, conceived and initiated by Collins, functioned as the catalyst event that separated these three operators permanently from institutional affiliation and created the conditions under which the Reapers subsequently formed. Collins is not assessed as having intended this outcome. He is not assessed as having any awareness of it.
Collins himself has been fully separated from the group since his arrest. He cut all ties following his court martial. He is currently classified as a peripheral historical figure in relation to the Reapers — not a member, not an asset, not in contact with any confirmed member. See: RELATED INDIVIDUALS section below.

CONFIRMED MEMBERS
LIBRA — File Ref: UETF-LS-63560-00
Role within group: Shadow-commander. Assessed as the primary strategic authority. No orders have been intercepted through any monitored channel. Operational sequencing and target selection across all confirmed Reapers incidents are attributed to this individual based on behavioral pattern analysis. Former PROJECT CONSTELLATION operative. Officially recorded KIA, Kazakhstan, 2031. Status corrected to confirmed alive and active as of December 2047.
Assessment note: Following the Kazakhstan incident, LIBRA's stated intent — confirmed through intercepts obtained during the UEJTF period — was to pursue Founder Rodriguez directly. Collins did not share this objective and did not participate beyond the Kazakhstan operation. LIBRA proceeded without him. The current campaign against ECAF infrastructure is assessed as the continuation of that objective by other means.
Full individual assessment on file: ECAF-CI-0047-LIBRA.
GAVALAS, ADRIAN — File Ref: UETF-PI-75038-00
Callsign: PITHOK
Role within group: Breaching and assault. Primary frontline direct-action asset. Exceptional physical capability documented across UEJTF service period. Confirmed tolerance for sustained close-quarters engagement. Last confirmed sighting: Rozhia, 13 November 2031. Official NATO status: KIA. Actual status: alive and active. Re-categorization pending formal corroboration.
Assessment note: Gavalas is officially listed as killed as a direct result of Collins's unauthorized deployment to Kazakhstan. This assessment is accurate in the sense that Collins's operation placed him at the facility. It is inaccurate in that Gavalas survived. Collins is not known to be aware of this. He has not been informed. Current behavioral monitoring of Collins indicates he continues to carry the deaths of Gavalas and Seale as confirmed losses attributable to his own decisions. No evidence suggests this belief has been corrected.
SEALE, LIAM — File Ref: UETF-SL-86004-02
Callsign: ORKA
Role within group: Overwatch and precision engagement. Long-range asset. Operates independently during field missions providing cover and target acquisition for the assault element. Last confirmed sighting: Rozhia industrial sector, 13 November 2031. Official NATO status: KIA. Actual status: alive and active. Re-categorization pending formal corroboration.
Assessment note: Same origin circumstances as Gavalas. Same note regarding Collins applies.

GROUP COMPOSITION ANALYSIS
The Reapers present a self-contained operational capability across the full spectrum of direct-action requirements. Confirmed membership accounts for strategic command (LIBRA), close-assault and breaching (PITHOK), and long-range precision overwatch (ORKA). The cell requires no external support structure to execute complex multi-phase operations. This is consistent with the observed pattern of attacks on ECAF infrastructure, which have demonstrated coordinated sequencing, advance reconnaissance, and clean extraction in all confirmed incidents.
All three confirmed members disappeared from NATO tracking networks following the Rozhia facility detonation on 13 November 2031. The convergence of their disappearances at the same location on the same date is not assessed as coincidental.
It is noted that the individual who connected these three operators — Collins — is not among them. He was extracted, tried, imprisoned, and discharged. The group that formed in his absence was built from the people his operation put in the same place at the same time. Whether LIBRA consolidated the remaining operators during or after the Kazakhstan incident has not been established.
Current confirmed membership: three. At least one additional operator deviated from extraction during the Rozhia incident before subsequently attempting to return to NATO territory and being arrested. Identity and current status of this individual is not addressed in this document and is restricted above current clearance.

OPERATIONAL PATTERN
Targets to date have been exclusively ECAF infrastructure assets. No civilian casualties have been attributed to group activity. Operations demonstrate advance knowledge of ECAF facility layouts, personnel schedules, and security protocols — consistent with LIBRA's assessed familiarity with ECAF's own monitoring and security doctrine.
Breach and assault phases in confirmed incidents are consistent with PITHOK's documented operational style. Overwatch elements in at least two confirmed incidents are consistent with ORKA's assessed capabilities. Strategic sequencing across all incidents is assessed as the product of a single coordinating intelligence, consistent with LIBRA's profile.
The group has sustained operations for an extended period without confirmed compromise of any member. No electronic communications attributable to any confirmed Reapers member have been intercepted.

RELATED INDIVIDUALS
COLLINS, SAMUEL — File Ref: UETF-CS-10923-00
Classification: AUTHORIZED. Person of interest. Not a Reapers member.
Collins is the individual whose unauthorized operation in 2031 directly produced the conditions that led to the Reapers' formation. He is not assessed as an active participant, associate, or point of contact for any confirmed group member in the current period. Following his release from Marshall Prison, Collins severed all ties with former UEJTF personnel and has maintained civilian status in NewLake City, Utah. He is currently under ECAF Level 5 surveillance.
Collins believes Gavalas and Seale were killed as a result of his decisions in Kazakhstan. This belief is, to ECAF's knowledge, uncorrected. He is not known to have had contact with LIBRA, Gavalas, or Seale since 2031. His connection to the Reapers is historical and involuntary.
He remains relevant to this file for two reasons. First, his actions created the group. Second, LIBRA's documented attachment to Collins — established during the UEJTF period and confirmed by intercepted audio post-Rozhia — has not been formally assessed as resolved. Whether LIBRA has made or intends to make contact with Collins in the current operational period is unknown. Any change in Collins's behavioral pattern, associations, or surveillance profile should be flagged to this division immediately.

THREAT ASSESSMENT
Group threat level: EXTREME.
Individual threat levels: see respective dossiers.
The combination of LIBRA's strategic capability and zero digital footprint, PITHOK's direct-action capacity, and ORKA's precision overwatch produces a cell that is operationally complete, self-sufficient, and highly resistant to standard surveillance methodology.
All confirmed members possess detailed knowledge of NATO and ECAF operational procedures, response protocols, and communication structures acquired during active UEJTF service. This knowledge is assessed as being actively applied to avoid detection and anticipate institutional countermeasures.
Engagement recommendation: Do not attempt interdiction with standard force complement. Any confirmed sighting of one or more Reapers members should be escalated immediately. Field personnel are directed to individual dossiers for specific engagement advisories.
Note regarding PITHOK: Physical dimensions (213 cm, 107 kg) combined with documented endurance and aggression metrics require specific tactical consideration in any close-quarters scenario, independent of the CQC advisory issued for LIBRA.
Note regarding ORKA: Subject's overwatch capability means any confirmed sighting of PITHOK or LIBRA in the field should be treated as a potential dual-threat scenario. Assume a precision element is active and unlocated until confirmed otherwise.

CROSS-REFERENCE FILES
UETF-LS-63560-00 — LIBRA
UETF-PI-75038-00 — GAVALAS, ADRIAN / PITHOK
UETF-SL-86004-02 — SEALE, LIAM / ORKA
UETF-CS-10923-00 — COLLINS, SAMUEL (related individual, not a member)
ECAF-TA-0047-LIBRA — Extended individual assessment
[REDACTED] — Fourth Rozhia operator, arrested. File restricted above current clearance.

END OF FILE
ECAF-CI-GRP-0091-REAPERS
This document is not acknowledged outside Tier 1 clearance. Unauthorized access or distribution is subject to ECAF Security Directive 9.

title: REAPERS — ROGUE OPERATIVE GROUP
body:
ENTITY CORPORATION ARMED FORCES
HERMES TACTICAL INTELLIGENCE DIVISION
DOCUMENT CLASS: RESTRICTED — TIER 1 ACCESS ONLY
ORIGINATING DIVISION: ECAF INTELLIGENCE DIVISION — THREAT ASSESSMENT BRANCH
FILE REF: ECAF-TA-00091-REAPERS
DATE CREATED: 22 DEC 2047
LAST UPDATED: 22 DEC 2047
REVIEW STATUS: ACTIVE — PRIORITY ESCALATION

SUBJECT: COLLECTIVE THREAT ASSESSMENT — ROGUE OPERATIVE GROUP
DESIGNATION: REAPERS
CLASSIFICATION: RESTRICTED
GROUP STATUS: ACTIVE — UNCONTAINED

FOREWORD — ESCALATION NOTICE
This assessment was formally initiated on 20 December 2047 following the confirmed identification of a previously KIA-listed operative as alive and active within the territory of NewLake City, Utah. The confirmation of this individual's survival necessitated a full reassessment of attribution for a series of incidents logged against ECAF infrastructure between 2045 and present date, previously unattributed.
This document consolidates all available intelligence pertaining to the group now designated REAPERS. It is the first group-level threat assessment filed against this cell. All prior incident reports filed under separate reference numbers are to be cross-referenced and appended to this file pending divisional review.
Individual dossiers for confirmed members are filed separately under their respective HERMES reference numbers. This document addresses group-level composition, origin, operational pattern, and threat classification only.

ORIGIN — THE ROZHIA INCIDENT AND KAZAKHSTAN, NOVEMBER 2031
The Reapers are assessed as a direct and unintended product of events originating on 13 November 2031 during UEJTF Team 11's final operational period.
On that date, three UEJTF operators — designated in NATO records as LIBRA, Gavalas Adrian, and Seale Liam — participated in an unauthorized assault on a suspected weapons manufacturing facility in the Rozhia industrial sector. The operation was initiated without command authorization by then-Master Sergeant Samuel Collins, who had mobilized a splinter element of Team 11 operators in pursuit of a target referred to internally as "Ash" and a suspected weapons system referred to as "The Core."
The facility detonated. Collins survived, was extracted, and subsequently court-martialed. The three remaining operators were listed as KIA in the immediate aftermath. A fourth operator attempted to return to NATO territory several days later and was arrested. That individual's identity and current status are restricted above the clearance level of this document.
Collins was tried before a NATO Joint Military Tribunal on 14 November 2031 and pleaded guilty to charges including insubordination, theft of military assets, and gross negligence resulting in the deaths of three NATO operatives. He served three years in Marshall Prison and was dishonorably discharged. He currently resides in NewLake City, Utah, under ECAF Level 5 civilian surveillance.
The three operators listed as KIA — LIBRA, Gavalas, and Seale — did not die.
Their survival was not reported to any NATO or ECAF authority. Each disappeared from all monitored networks independently following the incident and remained absent from all tracking infrastructure for a period of approximately fourteen years. The mechanism by which they survived, coordinated, and subsequently organized has not been established.

CONFIRMED MEMBERS
LIBRA
File Ref: UETF-LS-63560-00
Extended Assessment: ECAF-TA-00047-LIBRA
Role within group: Shadow-commander. Assessed as the primary strategic authority for all Reapers operations. No communications attributable to this individual have been intercepted through any monitored channel during the post-2031 period. Target selection, operational sequencing, and breach methodology across all confirmed Reapers incidents are attributed to this individual based on behavioral pattern analysis and cross-referenced against his documented operational history.
Former PROJECT CONSTELLATION operative. Officially recorded KIA, Kazakhstan facility, November 2031. Status corrected to confirmed alive and active following visual confirmation in NewLake City, Utah, 20 December 2047.
Assessment note: LIBRA's individual threat profile is the most extensive on record within this division. Field personnel are directed to ECAF-TA-00047-LIBRA prior to any operational engagement. The CQC advisory contained within that file is mandatory reading for all personnel assigned to any interdiction effort involving this individual.
GAVALAS, ADRIAN
File Ref: UETF-PI-75038-00
Callsign: PITHOK
Role within group: Direct action. Primary assault and breaching asset. Responsible for physical entry, frontline engagement, and close-quarters clearance in all operations where a breach element has been identified. Exceptional physical capability documented extensively across his UEJTF service record. Confirmed tolerance for sustained high-intensity combat and abnormal pain and fatigue threshold.
Official NATO status: KIA, Kazakhstan, November 2031. Actual status: alive and active. Formal re-categorization pending corroboration above current threshold.
Assessment note: Subject's physical dimensions — 213 cm, 107 kg — combined with his documented aggression metrics and breaching specialization require explicit tactical consideration independent of any other threat advisory issued for this group. In any scenario involving close-quarters engagement, Gavalas should be treated as a primary physical threat regardless of which other group members are confirmed present.
SEALE, LIAM
File Ref: UETF-SL-86004-02
Callsign: ORKA
Role within group: Overwatch and precision engagement. Long-range asset providing cover, target acquisition, and field intelligence during active operations. Assessed as operating independently from the assault element during engagements, maintaining an unlocated elevated or concealed position.
Official NATO status: KIA, Kazakhstan, November 2031. Actual status: alive and active. Formal re-categorization pending corroboration above current threshold.
Assessment note: Any confirmed sighting of LIBRA or PITHOK in a field environment should be treated as a dual-threat scenario until ORKA's position is confirmed and neutralized. Subject's documented overwatch endurance — sustained periods exceeding 18 hours without performance degradation — means absence of visual confirmation does not indicate absence of the asset.

GROUP COMPOSITION ANALYSIS
The Reapers present a self-contained and operationally complete capability across all direct-action requirements. Confirmed membership accounts for strategic command and planning (LIBRA), close assault and forced entry (PITHOK), and long-range precision overwatch (ORKA). No external support structure has been identified as necessary for the group to execute complex multi-phase operations. This is consistent with the pattern of all confirmed incidents attributed to the group, each of which demonstrated coordinated sequencing, advance reconnaissance, controlled escalation, and clean extraction.
The group's shared UEJTF background means all three confirmed members possess detailed working knowledge of NATO and ECAF operational procedures, response protocols, communication structures, and infrastructure layouts. This knowledge is assessed as being actively applied to anticipate and circumvent institutional countermeasures. The absence of any successful interception of group communications over a minimum two-year active operational period is consistent with this assessment.
Operational funding source: unidentified. No financial trail attributable to any confirmed group member has been recovered. No external sponsor has been identified. This division has no current basis on which to project resource limitations.

OPERATIONAL HISTORY
2031 — 2044: No confirmed activity. All three members absent from all monitored infrastructure. Whereabouts during this period unknown.
2045 — 2046: A series of incidents involving ECAF's early network infrastructure, at that time operating under the provisional designation FENRIR, were logged as unattributed intrusions. Incidents included unauthorized server access, confirmed data exfiltration of an undetermined volume, and the introduction of falsified intelligence products into active ECAF workflows. At the time of logging, these incidents were assessed as the work of an unidentified external hacker or insider threat. No group attribution was made. Affected files and compromised data sets are currently under audit. The full scope of what was accessed or altered during this period has not been determined.
Assessment note: The sophistication of the FENRIR intrusions is now assessed as consistent with an operator possessing detailed prior knowledge of the system's architecture. The volume of falsified intelligence successfully introduced into active ECAF workflows during this period without detection suggests the intrusion was not opportunistic. A targeted, patient, and technically capable actor is assessed as responsible. Attribution to the Reapers is assessed as probable but not yet formally confirmed.
2047: Operational activity escalated from network intrusion to direct physical engagement against ECAF assets. Specific incidents are documented under separate reference numbers pending consolidation into this file. All confirmed 2047 incidents demonstrate coordination between a breach element and a precision overwatch asset, consistent with PITHOK and ORKA operating in support of LIBRA's direction.

OBJECTIVE ASSESSMENT
The Reapers have not communicated demands. They have not claimed responsibility for any incident. They have not contacted any ECAF personnel, media outlet, or third party.
Based on the operational history of all confirmed members and the pattern of activity attributed to the group, this division's current assessment of their objective is as follows:
The group's campaign is not assessed as ideologically motivated. It is not assessed as financially motivated. It is assessed as specifically and personally directed at ECAF as an institution and at individuals within ECAF's senior structure whose actions during the 2031 operational period are assessed by the group as having been deliberately concealed from accountability.
The nature of what the group believes was concealed has not been fully established by this division. Their operational behavior suggests they are in possession of intelligence that ECAF has not been able to identify or recover. The FENRIR intrusion period of 2045–2046 is assessed as the most probable source of whatever intelligence is currently driving their targeting decisions.
This division notes that the group has not, to date, killed any ECAF personnel. Engagements have resulted in injuries and incapacitation but no confirmed fatalities attributable to Reapers activity. Whether this reflects operational restraint as a matter of policy, or a tactical choice specific to the current phase of their campaign, has not been determined.

RELATED INDIVIDUALS
COLLINS, SAMUEL
File Ref: UETF-CS-10923-00
Classification: AUTHORIZED. Person of interest. Not a confirmed Reapers member.
Collins is the individual whose unauthorized operation in November 2031 placed LIBRA, Gavalas, and Seale at the Kazakhstan facility and created the conditions from which the Reapers subsequently formed. He is not assessed as an active participant, associate, or current point of contact for any confirmed group member.
Following his release from Marshall Prison and dishonorable discharge, Collins severed all documented ties with former UEJTF personnel. He has maintained civilian status in NewLake City, Utah since that time, operating a licensed shooting range. He is currently under ECAF Level 5 surveillance with no anomalous activity recorded.
Collins is officially recorded as believing that Gavalas and Seale were killed as a direct result of his decisions in Kazakhstan. This belief is, to this division's knowledge, uncorrected. He has not been informed otherwise.
He remains relevant to this file for the following reason: LIBRA's documented attachment to Collins — established during the UEJTF period and confirmed by intercepted audio recovered following the Rozhia engagement — has not been assessed as resolved. Whether LIBRA has made or intends to make contact with Collins during the current operational period is unknown. Any deviation in Collins's behavioral baseline, associations, movement patterns, or communications profile is to be flagged to this division immediately and treated as a potential indicator of broader Reapers activity in the NewLake City area.
KHACHATURIAN, ARMAN
Armenian national. Criminal financier. Confirmed associate of the individual designated ASH during the 2031 Kovalov abduction. Relevance to current Reapers activity: under assessment. Cross-reference: NATI-AN-33518-00.
ASH
File Ref: UNKN-AS-95723-00
Cold file. Investigation terminated prior to HERMES founding. Relevance to current Reapers activity: under assessment. Note — intercepts from the 2031 UEJTF period confirm this designation was a primary target of the unsanctioned Collins operation. Whether the Reapers have independently identified or are actively pursuing this individual is unknown. File re-examination has been requested by this division and is pending authorization.

THREAT ASSESSMENT
Group threat level: EXTREME.
Containment status: UNCONTAINED.
Individual threat levels: see respective dossiers.
The Reapers represent a threat category this division has not previously assessed. The group is operationally self-sufficient, financially untraced, electronically silent, and in possession of an unknown quantity of ECAF's own intelligence. They have demonstrated the capability and patience to operate below detection threshold for a minimum of two years before escalating to direct engagement. They have now escalated.
All field personnel are advised: do not attempt interdiction with a standard force complement. Any confirmed sighting of one or more Reapers members is to be escalated through this division immediately before any field response is initiated.
The group knows ECAF's procedures. Plan accordingly.

CROSS-REFERENCE FILES
UETF-LS-63560-00 — LIBRA
UETF-PI-75038-00 — GAVALAS, ADRIAN / PITHOK
UETF-SL-86004-02 — SEALE, LIAM / ORKA
UETF-CS-10923-00 — COLLINS, SAMUEL (related individual)
USMC-NM-26701-05 — Marcus Novak
NATI-AN-33518-00 — KOVALOV, ANTON (related asset, KIA)
UNKN-AS-95723-00 — ASH (cold file, re-examination pending)
[REDACTED] — Fourth Rozhia operator, arrested. Restricted above current clearance.

END OF FILE
ECAF-TA-0047-LIBRA — Extended individual assessment
DATE: 22 DEC 2047
This document is not acknowledged outside Tier 1 clearance. Unauthorized access or distribution is subject to ECAF Security Directive 9.
